title: EaROS Reference Architecture Artifact
description: Schema for EaROS-governed Reference Architecture documents. Derived from the required_evidence fields of the core meta-rubric (EAROS-CORE-002) and the reference-architecture profile (EAROS-REFARCH-001). Sections map directly to rubric criteria so that a well-completed document generates the evidence an assessor needs. Derivation chain: Rubric criteria (required_evidence) → this schema (sections) → artifact document → evaluation record.

kind.title: Document kind
kind.description: Fixed discriminator value. Always 'artifact' for architecture documents. Distinguishes from 'core_rubric', 'profile', 'overlay', and 'evaluation' file kinds.

artifact_type.title: Artifact type
artifact_type.description: Locked to 'reference_architecture'. Binds this document to profile EAROS-REFARCH-001 alongside the core meta-rubric.

metadata.title: Document metadata
metadata.description: Administrative and identification fields. Maps to evidence required by core criteria STK-01 (purpose, stakeholders, decision_context) and MNT-01 (owner, version, last_updated, change_log). All required fields must be present for a score above 1 on MNT-01.

title.title: Title
title.description: Full title of the architecture document. Should identify the system or platform and the nature of the document. Example: 'Payments Platform Reference Architecture v2'.

version.title: Version
version.description: Semantic version of this artifact (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH). Increment MAJOR for breaking structural changes, MINOR for new sections or significant content updates, PATCH for corrections and clarifications.

status.title: Status
status.description: Lifecycle status: 'draft' (in progress, not yet reviewed), 'candidate' (ready for Architecture Board review), 'approved' (formally accepted), 'deprecated' (superseded by a newer version).

author.title: Author
author.description: Individual who created or last significantly updated this document. This is authorship credit, not ongoing ownership responsibility.

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: Team or individual with ongoing stewardship responsibility for this document. The owner keeps it current, triages feedback from adopting teams, and decides when to deprecate. Evidence for core criterion MNT-01. Example: 'Enterprise Architecture, Payments Domain'.

effective_date.title: Effective date
effective_date.description: Date from which this version is the active reference. Format: YYYY-MM-DD.

next_review_date.title: Next review date
next_review_date.description: Date by which this document must be reviewed and either reconfirmed or updated. Establishes a review cadence, which is evidence for MNT-01 score ≥ 3. Format: YYYY-MM-DD.

last_updated.title: Last updated
last_updated.description: Date of the most recent substantive change. Format: YYYY-MM-DD.

purpose.title: Purpose statement
purpose.description: One or two sentences stating what this document is for and what decisions it supports. Evidence for core criterion STK-01 (major gate). Example: 'This reference architecture defines the target pattern for all event-driven microservices on the Payments platform. It supports Architecture Board review and serves as the golden path for new service development.'

decision_context.title: Decision or usage context
decision_context.description: The governance or project context in which this document is being used — the Architecture Board review it supports, the programme it belongs to, or the migration decision it underpins. Evidence for core criterion STK-01.

stakeholders.title: Stakeholders
stakeholders.description: Named individuals or roles with a significant concern in this architecture. Each entry should identify who the stakeholder is and what concern this document addresses for them. Evidence for core criterion STK-01 (named stakeholder list required for score ≥ 2).

name.title: Name or team
name.description: Individual name or team name (e.g. 'Head of Payments', 'Security Architecture', 'Platform Team').

role.title: Role
role.description: Stakeholder's organisational role or relationship to this architecture.

concerns.title: Concerns addressed
concerns.description: What this stakeholder needs to understand from this document. Used to justify which views and sections are included. Evidence for core criterion STK-02 (concern-to-view mapping).

change_log.title: Change log
change_log.description: History of substantive changes to this document, most recent first. Provides provenance and supports governance traceability. Evidence for core criterion MNT-01 — a change log with at least one entry is required for MNT-01 score ≥ 3.

version.title: Version
version.description: Version number this entry corresponds to.

date.title: Date
date.description: Date this version was published. Format: YYYY-MM-DD.

changes.title: Changes
changes.description: List of substantive changes in this version. Each item should describe what changed and why.

sections.title: Document sections
sections.description: The substantive content of the architecture document, organised into assessment-aligned sections. Each section maps to one or more EAROS rubric criteria and their required_evidence fields. A section omitted or left empty will result in a lower score on the corresponding criterion.

reading_guide.title: Reading guide
reading_guide.description: Maps document sections to stakeholder concerns so readers know which parts address their specific interests. Evidence for core criterion STK-02. Having an explicit section-to-concern mapping is required for STK-02 score ≥ 3.

how_to_use.title: How to use this document
how_to_use.description: Brief narrative explaining the document's structure and how different readers should navigate it.

section_map.title: Section-to-concern mapping
section_map.description: Explicit table mapping each section to the audience and concern it addresses. A complete mapping with every section covered is evidence for STK-02 score 4.

section.title: Section
section.description: Name or number of the document section.

audience.title: Audience
audience.description: Stakeholder role(s) this section is primarily for.

concern.title: Concern addressed
concern.description: The specific stakeholder concern this section addresses.

scope.title: Scope and boundary
scope.description: Defines exactly what is and is not covered by this architecture. Without clear scope, the artifact may be rated 'not_reviewable'. Evidence for core criterion SCP-01 (critical gate — score < 2 triggers not_reviewable status).

statement.title: Scope statement
statement.description: A concise, unambiguous statement of what this reference architecture covers. Should name specific systems, services, or domains included.

in_scope.title: In scope
in_scope.description: Explicit list of systems, services, domains, or concerns that this architecture covers.

out_of_scope.title: Out of scope
out_of_scope.description: Explicit list of items intentionally excluded, with a brief rationale or reference to where they are addressed. Having explicit exclusions is required for SCP-01 score > 2. Example: 'Authentication — handled by IAM platform, see IAM-2024-001'.

boundary_definition.title: Boundary definition
boundary_definition.description: Description of the system context boundary — what is inside this architecture vs. what is external. Can reference or describe the C4 context diagram or equivalent boundary representation.

assumptions.title: Contextual assumptions
assumptions.description: Assumptions about the environment that define the conditions under which this scope is valid. Each assumption should state the consequence if it is violated. Evidence for SCP-01 score ≥ 3.

assumption.title: Assumption
assumption.description: The assumption being made. Example: 'Banking Core API versioned contract is stable for 12 months'.

consequence_if_violated.title: Consequence if violated
consequence_if_violated.description: What happens to this architecture if the assumption proves false.

drivers_and_principles.title: Drivers and principles
drivers_and_principles.description: Business drivers, requirements, and architecture principles that shaped this design. Evidence for core criterion TRC-01 (major gate). Drivers and principles listed here must be traceable to specific design elements throughout the document — listing them without connecting them to the architecture achieves only a score of 1.

drivers.title: Business drivers
drivers.description: The business motivations, problems, or opportunities that this architecture addresses. Each driver should have an explicit architecture_response linking it to a design element or decision. Evidence for TRC-01.

id.title: Driver ID
id.description: Short reference ID for traceability (e.g. 'DR-01'). Used by decisions and design elements to reference back to this driver.

description.title: Description
description.description: The business driver, requirement, or opportunity in concrete terms. Example: 'Reduce payment fraud rate by 30% within 12 months'.

architecture_response.title: Architecture response
architecture_response.description: How this architecture responds to or satisfies the driver. Reference specific sections, decisions, or components by name. This linkage is the evidence for TRC-01 score ≥ 2.

principles.title: Architecture principles
principles.description: Enterprise or domain architecture principles that constrain or guide design decisions. Principles cited here should be referenced in the rationale of architecture decisions throughout the document.

id.title: Principle ID
id.description: Reference ID from the enterprise architecture principles catalogue.

name.title: Name
name.description: Short name of the principle. Example: 'API-first', 'Zero-trust networking', 'Platform over bespoke'.

how_applied.title: How applied in this architecture
how_applied.description: How this principle manifests in this architecture. Reference specific design elements or decisions. This is the evidence required by TRC-01 for principles.

architecture_views.title: Architecture views
architecture_views.description: The set of views representing the reference architecture from different perspectives. Evidence for profile criterion RA-VIEW-01 (major gate) and RA-VIEW-02. RA-VIEW-01 score 2 requires three views; score 3 requires all four primary views with a data flow narrative; score 4 requires cross-references between views and a security view.

context.title: Context view
context.description: Shows how the system relates to its environment — external actors, upstream/downstream systems, and the system boundary. Equivalent to C4 Level 1. Required for RA-VIEW-01 score ≥ 2.

description.title: Description
description.description: Narrative explaining what this view shows and the key relationships with external systems and actors. Should identify who initiates interactions and what data flows at the boundary.

diagram_source.title: Diagram source
diagram_source.description: Diagram source code or image content. Preferred formats for RA-VIEW-02 scoring: Structurizr DSL, PlantUML, Mermaid, or ArchiMate exchange format. Image-only scores lower on RA-VIEW-02.

source_type.title: Source type
source_type.description: Format of the diagram source. 'structurizr', 'plantuml', or 'mermaid' enable diagram-as-code scoring for RA-VIEW-02.

functional.title: Functional / container view
functional.description: Shows the major structural components (containers, services, or modules) of the architecture and their relationships. Equivalent to C4 Level 2. Required for RA-VIEW-01 score ≥ 2.

description.title: Description
description.description: Narrative describing the structural decomposition and the responsibilities of each major component. Technology annotations should be visible.

diagram_source.title: Diagram source
diagram_source.description: Diagram source code. Preferred: Structurizr DSL, PlantUML, or Mermaid.

deployment.title: Deployment view
deployment.description: Shows infrastructure topology — how components are deployed across nodes, containers, cloud regions, or availability zones. Required for RA-VIEW-01 score ≥ 3.

description.title: Description
description.description: Narrative explaining the deployment topology, including infrastructure choices, multi-AZ or multi-region strategy, and key operational characteristics.

data_flow.title: Data flow view
data_flow.description: Describes how data moves through the system at runtime. Required for RA-VIEW-01 score ≥ 3. A diagram without a numbered walkthrough is not sufficient — narrative_steps are required evidence.

description.title: Description
description.description: Overview of the primary data flows and processing patterns, including key integration points and transformations.

narrative_steps.title: Numbered flow steps
narrative_steps.description: Step-by-step walkthrough of the primary data flow. Numbered steps are required evidence for RA-VIEW-01 — a diagram alone does not satisfy this criterion.

description.title: Description
description.description: What happens at this step — which component sends what data to which component, and any transformation or decision point.

security.title: Security view (optional — required for score 4)
security.description: Shows the security architecture: trust boundaries, authentication/authorisation mechanisms, data protection controls, and security domains. Optional but required for RA-VIEW-01 score 4.

description.title: Description
description.description: Narrative describing the security architecture, trust boundaries, and key controls.

element_catalog.title: Element catalog
element_catalog.description: Structured inventory of all named components in the architecture. Required evidence for RA-VIEW-02. Enables assessors to verify that diagram names, technology choices, and responsibilities are consistent across all views. The catalog is the single source of truth for component names.

name.title: Name
name.description: Canonical name for this component. Must match exactly how it appears in all diagrams — any discrepancy is evidence for a lower CON-01 score.

technology.title: Technology
technology.description: Specific technology, runtime, or platform used. Example: 'Node.js 20', 'PostgreSQL 16', 'Apache Kafka 3.x', 'AWS Lambda'.

responsibility.title: Responsibility
responsibility.description: What this component does in one or two sentences.

relationships.title: Key relationships
relationships.description: The most important dependencies or interactions with other named components. Use the canonical name of the related component.

decisions.title: Architecture decisions (ADRs)
decisions.description: Architecture Decision Records documenting the key design choices made in this reference architecture. Evidence for profile criterion RA-DEC-01 (major gate). Score 1: technology choices without rationale. Score 2: rationale without alternatives. Score 3: ADR format with alternatives and trade-offs. Score 4: full ADRs with revisit conditions.

id.title: Decision ID
id.description: Unique identifier for this decision. Example: 'ADR-001', 'D-07'.

title.title: Title
title.description: Short title summarising the decision. Example: 'Use event-driven architecture for inter-service communication'.

context.title: Context
context.description: The situation, constraint, or driver that makes this decision necessary. What problem does this decision solve? Reference driver IDs where applicable.

options.title: Options considered
options.description: Alternatives evaluated before choosing. Required for RA-DEC-01 score ≥ 3. Documenting only the chosen option without alternatives is an anti-pattern.

decision.title: Decision
decision.description: Which option was chosen. State the choice clearly.

rationale.title: Rationale
rationale.description: Why this option was chosen. Reference business drivers and architecture principles. Example: 'Option C selected because it satisfies DR-01 (fraud reduction) and aligns with the API-first principle'.

tradeoffs.title: Trade-offs accepted
tradeoffs.description: What is sacrificed or complicated by this decision. Required for RA-DEC-01 score ≥ 3. Example: 'Increased operational complexity in exchange for auditability and decoupling'.

consequences.title: Consequences
consequences.description: Follow-on work, constraints, or implications this decision creates for adopting teams.

revisit_conditions.title: Revisit conditions
revisit_conditions.description: Specific conditions under which this decision should be reconsidered. Required for RA-DEC-01 score 4. Example: 'Revisit if throughput exceeds 50K events/sec or if team lacks event sourcing experience'.

driver_refs.title: Driver references
driver_refs.description: IDs of business drivers from drivers_and_principles.drivers that motivated this decision. Cross-referencing decisions to drivers is evidence for TRC-01.

component_classification.title: Component classification
component_classification.description: Defines the prescriptiveness of each component — which elements are mandatory, recommended, or optional. Evidence for profile criterion RA-DEC-02. Score 2: mandatory and optional elements distinguished. Score 3: three-level classification with rationale. Score 4: customisation framework with decision trees for deviation.

components.title: Component mandate levels
components.description: Classification table mapping each major component or design pattern to its mandate level with rationale.

name.title: Component or pattern
name.description: Name of the component, pattern, or design choice being classified. Should match the name in the element catalog.

mandate_level.title: Mandate level
mandate_level.description: mandatory: all adopting teams must use this; recommended: should use unless there is a justified reason not to; optional: teams may use based on context.

rationale.title: Rationale
rationale.description: Why this component has this mandate level.

extension_points.title: Extension points
extension_points.description: Formally defined variation points where adopting teams may deviate from the reference architecture without seeking an exception. Evidence for RA-DEC-02 score ≥ 3.

description.title: Description
description.description: What can be varied at this point and the constraints on variation.

guidance.title: Guidance
guidance.description: Recommendations on how to use this extension point, including when deviation is acceptable.

quality_attributes.title: Quality attributes
quality_attributes.description: Measurable targets for each quality attribute relevant to this architecture. Evidence for profile criterion RA-QA-01 (major gate). Score 1: adjectives without numbers. Score 2: some measurable targets. Score 3: all material attributes with validation approaches. Score 4: automated fitness functions in CI/CD.

attribute.title: Quality attribute
attribute.description: Name of the quality attribute. Use concrete, assessable names — not adjectives. Examples: 'Availability', 'Latency P99', 'Throughput', 'Error rate', 'Recovery Time Objective (RTO)', 'Recovery Point Objective (RPO)'.

target.title: Measurable target
target.description: A specific, measurable number or threshold. Must not be an adjective — numbers required. Example: '99.95% monthly availability', 'P99 < 200ms under 10K concurrent users', '5000 TPS sustained'.

measurement.title: Measurement method
measurement.description: How this attribute is measured in practice. Example: 'synthetic probes every 30s via monitoring platform', 'APM P99 latency histogram from production traffic'.

validation_strategy.title: Validation strategy
validation_strategy.description: How compliance with this target is validated before and after deployment. Required for RA-QA-01 score ≥ 3. Example: 'Gatling load test with 10K concurrent users in staging environment before each release'.

fitness_function.title: Fitness function
fitness_function.description: Automated test or check that continuously validates this attribute in CI/CD pipeline. Required for RA-QA-01 score 4. Example: 'Gatling load test fails build if P99 > 200ms; chaos engineering run weekly via Gremlin'.

quality_scenario.title: Quality scenario
quality_scenario.description: TOGAF or SEI-style quality scenario describing the stimulus, source, environment, response, and response measure for this attribute.

operational_model.title: Operational model
operational_model.description: How the architecture is operated, monitored, scaled, and recovered in production. Evidence for profile criterion RA-OPS-01 (major gate). Score 2: monitoring and scaling described in principle with SLOs mentioned. Score 3: concrete monitoring dashboards, scaling policies, SLOs, basic DR. Score 4: complete runbooks, alerting configs, error budgets.

slos.title: Service Level Objectives
slos.description: Defined SLOs for this architecture. Required evidence for RA-OPS-01. Each SLO must have a specific numeric target.

name.title: SLO name
name.description: Name of the SLO. Example: 'API availability', 'P99 request latency', 'Successful request rate'.

measurement_window.title: Measurement window
measurement_window.description: Rolling window or period over which this SLO is measured. Example: 'rolling 30 days', 'calendar month'.

error_budget.title: Error budget
error_budget.description: The allowed failure budget derived from this SLO. Required for RA-OPS-01 score 4. Example: '43.8 minutes/month at 99.95% availability'.

monitoring.title: Monitoring strategy
monitoring.description: How the architecture is observed in production. Required evidence for RA-OPS-01.

strategy.title: Strategy
strategy.description: Overview of the observability approach: signal types covered (metrics, logs, traces), tooling, and integration with on-call processes.

metrics.title: Key metrics
metrics.description: The most important metrics to monitor for this architecture. Example: 'HTTP request rate', 'P99 latency', 'Kafka consumer lag', 'Database connection pool utilisation'.

dashboards.title: Dashboard reference
dashboards.description: Reference to dashboard templates or existing dashboards. Example: 'Grafana dashboard template at /ops/dashboards/payments-platform.json'.

alerting_rules.title: Alerting rules
alerting_rules.description: Alerting thresholds and escalation policy. Reference alert configuration files if available. Required for RA-OPS-01 score 4.

scaling.title: Scaling policies
scaling.description: How the architecture scales under load. Required evidence for RA-OPS-01.

policies.title: Scaling policies
policies.description: Specific scaling rules and triggers. Example: 'HPA targeting 70% CPU, min 3 / max 20 pods per service', 'Database read replicas added when primary CPU > 80%'.

capacity_planning_notes.title: Capacity planning notes
capacity_planning_notes.description: Current load profile, known capacity limits, and headroom before the next scaling tier is needed.

disaster_recovery.title: Disaster recovery
disaster_recovery.description: Recovery objectives and procedures. Required evidence for RA-OPS-01.

rto.title: Recovery Time Objective (RTO)
rto.description: Maximum acceptable time to restore service after an incident. Example: '4 hours'.

rpo.title: Recovery Point Objective (RPO)
rpo.description: Maximum acceptable data loss measured in time. Example: '15 minutes'.

failover_procedures.title: Failover procedures
failover_procedures.description: High-level description of the failover process. Reference runbook if available.

runbook_ref.title: Runbook reference
runbook_ref.description: Location of the DR runbook (e.g. Confluence page URL, ops/ directory path). Tested DR runbooks referenced here are evidence for RA-OPS-01 score 4.

implementation_artifacts.title: Implementation artifacts
implementation_artifacts.description: Runnable and deployable artifacts that teams use to instantiate this reference architecture. Evidence for profile criterion RA-IMP-01. Score 2: some IaC or API specs. Score 3: complete set of IaC, API specs, and CI/CD. Score 4: full golden path including scaffold template and working sample application.

iac_templates.title: Infrastructure-as-code templates
iac_templates.description: Terraform, CDK, Bicep, CloudFormation, or equivalent templates that deploy the architecture. Required evidence for RA-IMP-01.

location.title: Location
location.description: Repository path or URL to the template.

api_specifications.title: API specifications
api_specifications.description: OpenAPI, AsyncAPI, or equivalent service interface specifications. Required evidence for RA-IMP-01.

cicd_templates.title: CI/CD pipeline templates
cicd_templates.description: Pipeline workflow files for building and deploying the architecture. Required evidence for RA-IMP-01.

scaffold_template.title: Scaffold template
scaffold_template.description: A template (e.g. Backstage Software Template, Cookiecutter, Copier) for bootstrapping a new service from this reference architecture. Required for RA-IMP-01 score 4 — the golden path must be automatable.

sample_application.title: Sample application
sample_application.description: A working reference implementation demonstrating the architecture in practice. Required for RA-IMP-01 score 4.

getting_started.title: Getting started guide
getting_started.description: A tested, step-by-step guide for new teams adopting this reference architecture. Evidence for profile criterion RA-IMP-02. Score 3: tested guide with prerequisites and time estimate. Score 4: fully automated onboarding achieving < 1 hour to first deployment.

estimated_time_to_first_deployment.title: Estimated time to first deployment
estimated_time_to_first_deployment.description: How long a new team takes from zero to a running deployed service following this guide. Required evidence for RA-IMP-02. Example: '4 hours', '< 1 hour with Backstage template'.

prerequisites.title: Prerequisites
prerequisites.description: Tools, access permissions, and knowledge required before starting. Required evidence for RA-IMP-02 score ≥ 3.

steps.title: Steps
steps.description: Numbered steps for getting started, tested against a real environment. Required evidence for RA-IMP-02.

description.title: What to do
description.description: Clear instructions for this step, including expected outcome.

command.title: Command (if applicable)
command.description: Shell command or code snippet to run at this step.

troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting
troubleshooting.description: Common problems encountered when following this guide and their resolutions. Required evidence for RA-IMP-02 score ≥ 3.

symptom.title: Symptom
symptom.description: What the problem looks like to a new adopter.

raid.title: RAID log (Risks, Assumptions, Issues, Dependencies)
raid.description: Risks, design assumptions, constraints, and trade-offs. Evidence for core criterion RAT-01. Score 2: risks listed without mitigations. Score 3: risks with mitigations and owners; assumptions and constraints documented. Score 4: evidence-based trade-off analysis with accepted consequences explicit.

risks.title: Risks
risks.description: Known risks with mitigations and named owners. Required evidence for RAT-01. Generic statements such as 'there are risks that will need to be addressed' score 1.

description.title: Description
description.description: Specific risk in concrete terms — what could go wrong and the consequence if it materialises.

mitigation.title: Mitigation
mitigation.description: Specific action taken or planned to reduce this risk. Required for RAT-01 score ≥ 3.

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: Named individual or role responsible for monitoring and mitigating this risk. Required for RAT-01 score ≥ 3.

residual_risk.title: Residual risk
residual_risk.description: Risk level remaining after mitigation (high/medium/low) and acceptance decision.

assumptions.title: Design assumptions
assumptions.description: Assumptions made during design that could affect the architecture if they prove false. Distinct from scope assumptions — these are design-time assumptions about technical or organisational behaviour.

constraints.title: Constraints
constraints.description: Technical, legal, or organisational constraints that limit design choices. Required evidence for RAT-01.

tradeoffs.title: Trade-offs
tradeoffs.description: Explicit trade-offs accepted in this design. Required for RAT-01 score ≥ 3. Each entry must state what was sacrificed and what was gained — not just that a trade-off exists.

decision_ref.title: Decision reference
decision_ref.description: ADR ID where this trade-off was formalised.

governance.title: Governance and compliance
governance.description: Alignment with applicable architecture standards, policies, and mandatory controls. Evidence for core criterion CMP-01 (critical gate — score < 1 triggers Reject regardless of other scores). Compliance by assertion ('we comply with all standards') scores 1 at most — explicit control-to-design mappings are required.

applicable_standards.title: Applicable standards
applicable_standards.description: Standards, policies, and regulations relevant to this architecture. Required to move beyond score 0 for CMP-01.

name.title: Name
name.description: Full name of the standard or policy.

relevance.title: Relevance
relevance.description: Why this standard applies to this architecture.

compliance_mapping.title: Compliance mapping
compliance_mapping.description: Explicit mapping from each applicable control to the design element that satisfies it. Required for CMP-01 score ≥ 3. Each entry links a specific control to the specific design component or decision that addresses it.

control.title: Control
control.description: Specific control reference. Example: 'ESS-01: mutual TLS for service-to-service calls'.

design_element.title: Design element
design_element.description: The component, decision, or design pattern that satisfies this control.

evidence.title: Evidence location
evidence.description: Where in this document the supporting evidence can be found.

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: Who is accountable for maintaining this compliance.

exceptions.title: Compliance exceptions
exceptions.description: Approved exceptions to mandatory controls. Each exception must have an approver, approval date, and either an expiry date or a remediation plan. Required for CMP-01 score 4.

remediation_plan.title: Remediation plan
remediation_plan.description: Plan to achieve full compliance, if expiry date is not set.

decisions_and_actions.title: Decisions and next actions
decisions_and_actions.description: The governance outcome and follow-on actions. Evidence for core criterion ACT-01. Score 2: some decisions stated. Score 3: key decisions made with owned actions. Score 4: all decisions explicit with owned, dated actions and clear governance path.

governance_outcome.title: Governance outcome
governance_outcome.description: The formal governance outcome for this architecture. Must be explicit — leaving this implicit is an anti-pattern for ACT-01.

decision_statement.title: Decision statement
decision_statement.description: A concise statement of what is approved or proposed. Example: 'Approve this reference architecture as the mandatory pattern for all event-driven microservices in the Payments domain, effective Q2 2026'.

conditions.title: Conditions (for conditional approval)
conditions.description: Named conditions that must be met for conditional approval to convert to full approval.

next_actions.title: Next actions
next_actions.description: Specific follow-on tasks with named owners and target dates. Required for ACT-01 score ≥ 3. Every action must have an owner.

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: Named individual or team responsible. No unowned actions.

review_triggers.title: Review triggers
review_triggers.description: Conditions that trigger a mandatory re-review of this architecture. Example: 'Architecture Board checkpoint 2026-04-10; post-migration assessment when migration completes'.

glossary.title: Glossary
glossary.description: Canonical definitions for key terms, component names, and abbreviations used in this document. Evidence for core criterion CON-01 (internal consistency). A glossary that is consistently used throughout the document is required for CON-01 score 4.

aliases.title: Aliases
aliases.description: Alternative names that refer to the same thing. Listing aliases here flags them as potential inconsistencies if found used differently elsewhere.

evolution.title: Evolution and lifecycle
evolution.description: How this reference architecture evolves over time. Evidence for profile criterion RA-REU-01. Score 2: versioned with change log. Score 3: known limitations, evolution plan, and feedback channel. Score 4: full lifecycle management with deprecation strategy and migration guidance.

known_limitations.title: Known limitations
known_limitations.description: Documented gaps or areas where this architecture does not yet meet its intended standard. Required for RA-REU-01 score ≥ 3.

evolution_roadmap.title: Evolution roadmap
evolution_roadmap.description: Planned improvements to this reference architecture, prioritised by value. Required for RA-REU-01 score ≥ 3.

deprecation_strategy.title: Deprecation strategy
deprecation_strategy.description: How this version will be deprecated when a successor is published, including migration guidance and transition timeline. Required for RA-REU-01 score 4.

feedback_channel.title: Feedback channel
feedback_channel.description: Where adopting teams report issues or request changes. Example: 'GitHub Issues on ThomasRohde/EAROS', 'Slack #platform-architecture channel'. Required for RA-REU-01 score ≥ 3.

migration_guidance.title: Migration guidance
migration_guidance.description: Guidance for teams migrating from a previous version of this reference architecture. Required for RA-REU-01 score 4 when breaking changes exist between versions.
